entries.title: Republic and Jedi Are Ancient Institutions

content.fact: In Legends, the Republic and Jedi Order are already ancient by most Old Republic stories; they should not feel newly invented during KOTOR or SWTOR-era scenes.
content.injection: In Old Republic scenes, treat the Republic and Jedi as long-standing institutions with accumulated precedent, bureaucracy, temples, and institutional memory.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Ancient Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Old Republic and Jedi Foundations.
antiLore: Do not portray the Jedi Order as a small new sect during KOTOR or SWTOR-era play.
Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends Old Republic material covering Tales of the Jedi, Knights of the Old Republic, KOTOR II, SWTOR-adjacent eras, ancient Sith/Jedi state, and late Old Republic transition context before the prequel-era Republic.

content.fact: The Sith tradition in Legends descends from a catastrophic Jedi schism and the fusion of exiled dark Jedi with Sith culture.
content.injection: For ancient Sith context, frame Sith identity as a legacy of exiled dark Jedi, Sith species culture, dark-side hierarchy, and revenge against the Republic and Jedi.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Ancient Sith Foundations.
Respect story-position bounds from Hundred-Year Darkness and Sith Schism to Hundred-Year Darkness and Sith Schism.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Old Sith Empire Is Not Rule of Two

content.fact: Ancient Sith societies field many Sith Lords and rival power structures; the Rule of Two is not active in this era.
content.injection: Before the Bane/Ruusan transition, do not reduce Sith politics to one master and one apprentice; write Sith space as aristocratic, factional, and full of competing dark-side elites.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Ancient Sith Foundations.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Sith Empire Ascendant to Great Hyperspace War.
antiLore: Do not apply Darth Bane’s Rule of Two to Exar Kun, KOTOR, or SWTOR-era Sith.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Great Hyperspace War Exposes Sith Space

content.fact: The Great Hyperspace War reveals the ancient Sith Empire to the wider Republic and makes Sith vengeance a recurring galactic threat.
content.injection: During Great Hyperspace War context, treat Sith contact as a galaxy-shaking shock: remote Sith space becomes a direct strategic and religious threat to the Republic and Jedi.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Great Hyperspace War.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Hyperspace War to Great Hyperspace War.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

content.fact: Sith holocrons can preserve old doctrine, personalities, temptations, and forbidden techniques across eras.
content.injection: Use Sith holocrons as dangerous continuity carriers: they can teach old secrets, lure ambitious Force-users, and bridge ancient Sith material into later Old Republic scenes.
constraints: Access should usually be limited, guarded, or corruptive rather than casual.
Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Ancient Sith Foundations.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Sith Empire Ascendant to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not let general model memory override the selected Legends story position.

entries.title: Exar Kun and Ulic Turn War Personal

content.fact: The Great Sith War is driven by fallen Jedi charisma, Sith artifacts, personal corruption, and the Republic-Jedi response to Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma.
content.injection: In Great Sith War scenes, keep the conflict personal and ideological: gifted Jedi fall, Sith relics matter, and old friendships become political and spiritual wounds.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Great Sith War.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Sith War Begins to Exar Kun Defeated on Yavin IV.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Nomi Sunrider Survives as Jedi Leader

content.fact: Nomi Sunrider becomes a major Jedi survivor and moral counterweight after the Great Sith War’s betrayals.
content.injection: After Exar Kun’s defeat, write Nomi Sunrider as a central Jedi figure carrying grief, authority, and the consequences of Ulic’s fall.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Great Sith War.
Respect story-position bounds from Exar Kun Defeated on Yavin IV to Exar Kun Defeated on Yavin IV.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Yavin Temples Hold Exar Kun Shadow

content.fact: Exar Kun’s defeat leaves a dangerous Sith shadow tied to Yavin IV in Legends continuity.
content.injection: After the Great Sith War, Yavin IV can carry dormant Sith danger; do not treat its temples as harmless archaeology if Exar Kun material is relevant.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Great Sith War.
Respect story-position bounds from Exar Kun Defeated on Yavin IV to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
